jokes.text: D'oh!
jokes.setup: Homer's signature catchphrase opening the episode
jokes.punchline: The familiar frustrated grunt
jokes.explanation: Iconic character moment but not particularly creative in this context

jokes.text: If I ever stop loving violence, I want you to shoot me. - Will do.
jokes.setup: Children casually discussing violence while watching cartoons
jokes.punchline: Lisa's matter-of-fact agreement to potentially murder her brother
jokes.explanation: Dark humor from children delivered with perfect casual tone, well-structured

jokes.text: Oh! Why can't they just take the gun?
jokes.setup: Marge insists Homer accompany the kids for safety
jokes.punchline: Homer suggests arming children as the easier solution
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer logic - missing the point entirely with dark humor

jokes.text: Perhaps because you are a prepubescent ignoramus.
jokes.setup: Bart asks why he's never seen a particular cartoon
jokes.punchline: Comic Book Guy's needlessly verbose insult
jokes.explanation: Typical Comic Book Guy condescension with unnecessarily complex vocabulary

jokes.text: Are you the creator of Hi and Lois? Because you are making me laugh.
jokes.setup: Bart offers $10 for a valuable drawing
jokes.punchline: Comparison to a notoriously unfunny comic strip
jokes.explanation: Clever reference requiring comic strip knowledge, well-executed sarcasm

jokes.text: I simply can't allow you to waste them here when there are so many crimes going unsolved at this very moment. Go! Go, for the good of the city.
jokes.setup: Comic Book Guy sarcastically compliments Bart's deduction skills
jokes.punchline: Dramatically urging him to fight crime instead of shopping
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of sarcasm with dramatic flair typical of the character

jokes.text: Oh, nerts!
jokes.setup: Bart's reaction to missing the parade
jokes.punchline: Using an old-fashioned mild expletive
jokes.explanation: Mild character moment, old-timey curse fits the cartoon theme

jokes.text: Which one's Itchy- the car? - The mouse.
jokes.setup: Homer asking about the parade float characters
jokes.punchline: Confusing a cartoon mouse with a car
jokes.explanation: Classic Homer confusion, simple but effective character comedy

jokes.text: Oh, isn't this just the most fun you've ever had in your life, Dave? - Yes, Suzanne. It is.
jokes.setup: TV anchors commenting on the parade
jokes.punchline: Deadpan agreement to obviously forced enthusiasm
jokes.explanation: Satirizes forced TV enthusiasm with deadpan delivery

jokes.text: Certainly seems to be a poorly planned parade route.
jokes.setup: Parade enters dangerous neighborhood
jokes.punchline: Understated observation of obvious disaster
jokes.explanation: Classic understatement humor as chaos unfolds

jokes.text: Get outta Bumtown, ya no-talent bum! Show some respect, man. That 'no-talent' created Itchy and Scratchy.
jokes.setup: Bumtown resident attacking Chester
jokes.punchline: Bart defending him while repeating the insult
jokes.explanation: Nice ironic structure with Bart accidentally repeating the insult while defending

jokes.text: When I complained, his thugs kicked me out of his office and dropped an anvil on me. Luckily I was carrying an umbrella at the time.
jokes.setup: Chester explains how he was mistreated
jokes.punchline: Cartoon logic applied to real violence - umbrella protects from anvil
jokes.explanation: Brilliant blend of cartoon physics with real-world story, very original

jokes.text: Before I came along, all cartoon animals did was play the ukulele. I changed all that.
jokes.setup: Chester claims to have invented cartoon violence
jokes.punchline: The idea that all early cartoons were just ukulele playing
jokes.explanation: Absurd but believable alternate history of animation, very quotable

jokes.text: Well, I'm not callin' you a liar, but- but I can't think of a way to finish that sentence.
jokes.setup: Bart starts to diplomatically question Chester
jokes.punchline: Admits he can't complete the thought without calling him a liar
jokes.explanation: Perfect misdirection with honest confusion, very relatable and quotable

jokes.text: Look out, Itchy.! He's Irish.!
jokes.setup: Watching old cartoon with ethnic stereotypes
jokes.punchline: Irish heritage treated as inherently dangerous
jokes.explanation: Commentary on old cartoon stereotypes, moderately effective

jokes.text: Shh.! That's Teddy Roosevelt.
jokes.setup: Bart calls a character fat
jokes.punchline: Reverent correction that it's a historical president
jokes.explanation: Good historical reference gag with appropriate reverence

jokes.text: That was a nice film I had once. Last time I try to impress a four-year-old.
jokes.setup: Chester's priceless historical film burns up
jokes.punchline: Casual dismissal of massive loss, blaming child audience
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing resigned acceptance and misplaced blame

jokes.text: He's a good man. Every Christmas he goes down to the pound and rescues one cat and one mouse and gives them to a hungry family.
jokes.setup: Bart explaining why Roger Myers Jr. is generous
jokes.punchline: Giving live cat and mouse to hungry people (implying they'll eat them)
jokes.explanation: Dark humor disguised as charity, connects to show themes

jokes.text: Hmm, studio's closed until Tuesday. Animators have A.A. on Monday.
jokes.setup: Explaining why the studio is closed
jokes.punchline: Animators are all in Alcoholics Anonymous
jokes.explanation: Industry satire suggesting creative types have drinking problems

jokes.text: Do you know what radon is? - No. - Good night.
jokes.setup: Bart offering Chester a place to sleep in basement
jokes.punchline: Casual acceptance of potentially dangerous living conditions
jokes.explanation: Dark humor about child's casual attitude toward health hazards

jokes.text: He's not a regular bum, Mom. He's a genius bum.
jokes.setup: Marge questioning the stranger in basement
jokes.punchline: Bart's classification system for homeless people
jokes.explanation: Child's logic creating categories, good character moment for Bart

jokes.text: $800 billion.
jokes.setup: Lawyer asks how much Chester expects
jokes.punchline: Ridiculously large sum for cartoon character theft
jokes.explanation: Absurd escalation of damages, fits character's delusional expectations

jokes.text: Oh, they got this all screwed up.
jokes.setup: Reading his own business card about 'No money down'
jokes.punchline: His own ad is misleading due to punctuation
jokes.explanation: Clever wordplay gag about punctuation changing meaning, perfect for Hutz

jokes.text: No, money down! Oops. I shouldn't have this Bar Association logo here either.
jokes.setup: Correcting the punctuation on his business card
jokes.punchline: Revealing he's not actually licensed
jokes.explanation: Perfect escalation revealing deeper incompetence, classic Hutz

jokes.text: I gave you a plate of corn muffins back in 1947 to paint my chicken coop. And you never did it! - Those corn muffins were lousy. - Paint my chicken coop! - Make me!
jokes.setup: Grandpa recognizes Chester from decades ago
jokes.punchline: Escalating argument over 50-year-old corn muffin debt
jokes.explanation: Absurd long-term grudge with escalating childish argument, good character work

jokes.text: That does it. One of them has to go. - Okay. Grandpa. - No, the B-U-M. - Oh.
jokes.setup: Marge declares someone has to leave
jokes.punchline: Homer immediately assumes it's Grandpa, not the stranger
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer logic and family dynamics, good misdirection

jokes.text: Brown-Nose Bear, Disgruntled Goat, Flatulent Fox, Rich Uncle Skeleton, and Dinner Dog.
jokes.setup: Listing other Itchy & Scratchy characters
jokes.punchline: Increasingly ridiculous character names, especially 'Dinner Dog'
jokes.explanation: Creative parody names with escalating absurdity, 'Dinner Dog' is particularly dark

jokes.text: I gave him a couple of blintzes to paint my fence- but he never did it! - Those blintzes were terrible. - Paint my fence! - Make me.
jokes.setup: Another character has same history with Chester
jokes.punchline: Identical pattern with different food and chore
jokes.explanation: Effective callback but less impact than original version

jokes.text: I gave him a couple of blintzes to paint my fence... but he never did it! - Those blintzes were terrible. - Paint my fence! - Make me.
jokes.setup: Similar story to Grandpa's complaint
jokes.punchline: Same pattern with different food and chore
jokes.explanation: Good callback structure but slightly repetitive - establishes Chester's pattern

jokes.text: Order! Order! We don't care about your blintzes.
jokes.setup: Court derailed by food-based argument
jokes.punchline: Judge's exasperated declaration about irrelevant blintzes
jokes.explanation: Good straight-man reaction to absurd courtroom tangent

jokes.text: If I hear 'objection' and 'sustained' one more time today, I think I'm going to scream. - Objection. - Sustained.
jokes.setup: Judge expresses frustration with legal formalities
jokes.punchline: Immediate repetition of the exact phrases
jokes.explanation: Perfect timing and structure for instant ironic payoff

jokes.text: The only characters Myers could ever come up with were pathetic stick figures with the words 'Sarcastic Horse' and 'Manic Mailman' printed on 'em.
jokes.setup: Chester describing Roger Myers Sr's lack of talent
jokes.punchline: Literal character names written on stick figures
jokes.explanation: Funny concept of lazy character design with literal labeling

jokes.text: Except for Flatulent Fox. That was based on a true story.
jokes.setup: Myers defending his father's character creations
jokes.punchline: Claiming a flatulent cartoon fox is biographical
jokes.explanation: Absurd deadpan delivery suggesting cartoon character has real basis

jokes.text: Well, Your Honor, we've got plenty of hearsay and conjecture. Those are... kinds of evidence.
jokes.setup: Judge asks for evidence
jokes.punchline: Hutz confidently presenting non-evidence as evidence
jokes.explanation: Perfect Hutz incompetence with confident delivery, very quotable

jokes.text: Your moxie more than makes up for your lack of talent. Your pal, ChesterJ. Lampwick.
jokes.setup: Reading encouraging inscription on drawing
jokes.punchline: Backhanded compliment about lack of talent
jokes.explanation: Good ironic evidence - supportive message that proves theft

jokes.text: Animation is built on plagiarism. If it weren't for someone plagiarizing The Honeymooners we wouldn't have The Flintstones. If someone hadn't ripped off Sergeant Bilko, there'd be no Top Cat.
jokes.setup: Myers defending stealing characters
jokes.punchline: Citing real examples of cartoon plagiarism
jokes.explanation: Brilliant meta-commentary on actual animation industry practices

jokes.text: Huckleberry Hound? Chief Wiggum? Yogi Bear? Ha! Andy Griffith. Edward G. Robinson. Art Carney.
jokes.setup: Continuing the plagiarism argument
jokes.punchline: Including Chief Wiggum (from this very show) in the list
jokes.explanation: Self-aware joke acknowledging The Simpsons' own voice-acting inspirations

jokes.text: Your Honor, you take away our right to steal ideas where are they gonna come from- her? Uh- hmm- How 'bout... Ghost Mutt?
jokes.setup: Myers asks where new ideas will come from
jokes.punchline: Lisa's weak suggestion proves his point
jokes.explanation: Good setup with Lisa's attempt actually supporting the opposition

jokes.text: I hope you're happy, kid. The studio's bankrupt. You just killed Itchy and Scratchy.
jokes.setup: Myers blaming Bart for the lawsuit result
jokes.punchline: Making kid responsible for ending beloved cartoon
jokes.explanation: Good dramatic irony - justice has unintended consequences

jokes.text: As for the rest of it, I'm gonna buy the one thing I've been dreaming of all the years I spent in the gutter: a solid gold house.
jokes.setup: Chester explaining what he'll do with his money
jokes.punchline: Impractical and gaudy dream purchase
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing poor judgment despite windfall

jokes.text: I'm an amendment to be Yes, an amendment to be And I'm hopin' that they'll ratify me
jokes.setup: Constitutional amendment singing about itself
jokes.punchline: Parody of Schoolhouse Rock with disturbing content
jokes.explanation: Clever parody setup for darker political commentary

jokes.text: There's a lot of flag burners Who have got too much freedom I wanna make it legal for policemen to beat 'em
jokes.setup: Educational cartoon format
jokes.punchline: Authoritarian message in cheerful children's song style
jokes.explanation: Dark political satire disguised as educational content

jokes.text: It's one of those campy, '70s throwbacks that appeals to Generation X'ers. We need another Vietnam to thin out their ranks a little.
jokes.setup: Grandpa commenting on the educational cartoon
jokes.punchline: Suggesting war to reduce generational competition
jokes.explanation: Dark generational humor typical of Grandpa's wartime perspective

jokes.text: Then I'll crush all opposition to me And I'll make Ted Kennedy pay If he fights back I'll say that he's gay
jokes.setup: Amendment continuing its authoritarian song
jokes.punchline: Threatening real politician with homophobic tactics
jokes.explanation: Political satire but relies on dated homophobic humor

jokes.text: So it's true. Some cartoons do encourage violence.
jokes.setup: Kids fighting after watching educational cartoon
jokes.punchline: Marge's ironic observation about cartoon influence
jokes.explanation: Good ironic callback to episode's themes about cartoon violence

jokes.text: I can't even keep my dad's head in the freakin' cryogenic center anymore.
jokes.setup: Myers explaining his financial problems
jokes.punchline: Casual mention of preserving father's severed head
jokes.explanation: Absurd and dark throwaway line delivered casually

jokes.text: I don't need any more money. I'm not greedy. As long as I've got my health and my millions of dollars and my gold house and my rocket car I don't need anything else.
jokes.setup: Chester claiming he's not greedy
jokes.punchline: List of increasingly extravagant possessions
jokes.explanation: Good escalation showing character's transformed priorities

jokes.text: You've even foiled Sideshow Bob on five separate occasions, and he's an evil genius.
jokes.setup: Marge encouraging kids by listing their achievements
jokes.punchline: Reference to recurring show villain and multiple episodes
jokes.explanation: Good meta-reference to show continuity and Bob's status

jokes.text: He discovered that the Postal Service's Mr. Zip was just a rip-off of my father's stick figure character Manic Mailman.
jokes.setup: Myers explaining how the problem was solved
jokes.punchline: Government character stolen from terrible stick figure
jokes.explanation: Good callback to earlier joke about Myers Sr's bad characters

jokes.text: What the hell is going on? - I don't know. But it looks like you might have a little competition all of a sudden.
jokes.setup: Bart and Lisa confused by being replaced
jokes.punchline: Lisa's observation about new problem-solving kids
jokes.explanation: Good meta-commentary on being upstaged by new characters

jokes.text: This is a great vindication for anybody who was ever taking a bath went to get the paper, fell down and had the door slam behind them and the doorknob break off.
jokes.setup: Chester giving speech about vindication
jokes.punchline: Extremely specific and unlikely scenario
jokes.explanation: Absurdly specific situation that somehow feels relatable

jokes.text: Hey, great. Listen. Write it down and mail it to last week, when I might have cared.
jokes.setup: Bart offers their solution after problem is solved
jokes.punchline: Sarcastic dismissal with time travel impossibility
jokes.explanation: Sharp sarcastic putdown with clever impossible suggestion

jokes.text: Well, technically everything worked out all right, but- But? Well, I wasn't the one who solved the problem and neither was Lisa. There's something unsettling about that.
jokes.setup: Marge asks if Bart is happy about the resolution
jokes.punchline: Bart's existential crisis about not being the hero
jokes.explanation: Meta-commentary on narrative expectations and character roles in sitcoms
